title: .github/workflows/ci_lesson_quality.yml

headings.text: # L5-04, L5-05, L5-06, L5-07 — EduBoost SA Phase 5
CI workflow: AI Lesson Quality — runs on every PR touching the lessons
module and after every lesson generation batch merge to master.
1. lesson-threshold-check   (L5-04) — ≥8 approved lessons per launch caps_ref
2. lesson-integrity-check   (L5-05) — all approved lessons pass validators
3. lesson-regression-suite  (L5-06) — re-validate on prompt version change

title: P1-07 — .github/workflows/migration_check.yml

headings.text: 1. Spins up a fresh PostgreSQL container
2. Runs `alembic upgrade head` from a completely empty database
3. Runs `alembic check` to verify no model drift exists
4. Runs `seed_item_bank.py --dry-run` to validate the seed file schema
# This job is required to pass before any PR that touches:
Then reference it as a required check in the branch protection rule.

top_headings: 1. Spins up a fresh PostgreSQL container

headings.text: EduBoost SA V2 — Agent TODO List
Target: Raise overall project score from 7.4 → 10.0 / 10
Phase 5 Complete | Groups A–I Complete | Group J release verification pending
- Follow TDD loop: write failing test → implement → green → commit
- A task is only DONE when: code ✅ | tests ✅ | docs ✅ | committed ✅
# TASK ORDER: Tasks are sequenced so each group builds on the previous.
Do NOT reorder within a group. Groups may be parallelised across agents.
GROUP A — CI/CD & REPOSITORY HYGIENE  (Score impact: CI/CD 8→10, DX 8→10)
GROUP B — SECURITY HARDENING  (Score impact: Security 7.0→10.0)

P0: POPIA Consent Lifecycle Is Wired to Incompatible Runtime Components

title: EduBoost V2 Recent Changes Technical Audit

headings.text: EduBoost V2 Recent Changes Technical Audit
1. Auth router boundary improved, but the service is still transitional
2. JWT keyring introduces rotation support but has an unsafe fallback
3. POPIA lifecycle wiring is safer but not response-contract proven
5. ARQ job repair is incomplete at dependency/runtime level
6. Lesson object authorization is materially improved but should be hardened

title: EduBoost SA Issues and Solutions Report

headings.text: EduBoost SA Issues and Solutions Report
REPO-001 - Dirty worktree obscures audit and release state
REPO-002 - Large file-mode drift across tracked files
REPO-003 - Untracked files affect import behavior and audit completeness
REPO-004 - `app.api.judiciary` package shadows untracked module file
REPO-005 - Runtime artifact is tracked in source control
ENV-001 - Local Python version does not match project pin
ENV-002 - Backend tests cannot run because pytest is missing locally
ENV-003 - `requirements.txt` contains duplicate and conflicting dependency declarations
ENV-004 - SQLite test URL requires missing `aiosqlite`
ENV-005 - Heavy ML dependencies are in the base install path
ENV-006 - Frontend dependency tree is locally inconsistent
ENV-007 - Frontend tests are blocked by missing Rollup optional native package
DB-001 - Alembic has split root revisions
DB-002 - Alembic migrations define duplicate `study_plans` tables

title: Makefile Duplicate Target Patch

headings.text: Recommendation 4: Remove duplicate Makefile target definitions.
# Five targets are defined twice.  In each case the SECOND definition is the
one that Make actually uses (later definitions win), so the FIRST occurrence
of each is removed.  Verify which copy is canonical before applying.
# Duplicates and their line positions (as of the 2026-05-12 assessment):
# The patch below removes the FIRST (earlier) occurrence of each duplicate.
# NOTE: Because the Makefile is 681 lines and context lines shift with each
removal, apply hunks individually or use the Python script which handles
